← all tutorials

Causal Consistency (COPS, Bayou) — interactive CAUSAL_CONSISTENCY.md causal_consistency.py

If A → B (A happens-before B), every replica must reveal A before B. Concurrent ops may appear in any order. Toggle R2 between a valid ordering and a causal violation; step through the dependency-check wait queue that saves the pathological [B, A] arrival. Everything recomputed in JS from the identical rules as causal_consistency.py, gold-checked at the bottom.  |  version vectors →

① The 4 operations and their causal dependencies

What to do: click an operation to see its dependency set and version vector. Solid arrows are causal links (A→B); ops with no arrow between them are concurrent (‖) — replicas may reorder them freely.
op (clickable) selected causal link A→B concurrent ‖
Click an operation to inspect its dependency set and version vector.

② Three replicas — valid orderings vs a causal violation

What to do: each replica reveals the same 4 ops in a different order. R0, R1 are causally valid (the two chains appear in different orders, but no reply precedes its post). Toggle R2: the violating order reveals B before its causal ancestor A; the fixed order reorders them. The red dashed arrow marks the reversed causal edge.
causal edges: A→B, C→D

③ Dependency check + wait queue — the same [B, A] arrival, handled correctly

This is the killer demo: the arrival order [B, A] that broke R2 in ② is now handled by the dep check. B arrives first but its dep {alice:1} isn't satisfied → it parks in the pending queue. When A arrives, B is flushed and applied. Step or auto-play.
step 0 / 3

④ The ladder — eventual < causal < linearizable

Pick a model and see what it does with the same [B, A] arrival (B before its causal ancestor A). Each step up the ladder adds a guarantee and a coordination cost.
check: … JS recompute matches causal_consistency.py GOLD (vectors, HB graph, schedule validity, dep-check delivery).
